expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: search box selector dropdown planning covers OK or Cancel confirmation, pending selection, and dismissal outcomes
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the output explicitly enumerates all required focus areas: “OK vs Cancel”, “pending (unconfirmed) selection state”, and “dismissal outcomes”, including specific dismissal modes (“click outside, ESC, navigation/blur”) and expectations like “popover does not dismiss unexpectedly during selection/loading.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly frames the deliverable as Phase 4a: “This benchmark case checks whether Phase 4a planning…”, and the “Phase alignment (Phase 4a)” section states it is “scenario-focused and subcategory-first” with “atomic action steps and observable outcomes,” matching the phase4a contract described in execution_notes.md (“scenario-level, atomic-step drafting (subcategory-first) … without Phase 4b canonical grouping”).
